shortAnswer: WBC (white blood cells) are the cells of your immune system that fight infections. High levels may indicate an infection or inflammation, while low levels can signal a weakened immune response. Always discuss abnormal values with your doctor.
metaDescription: WBC / white blood cells high or low? Normal ranges, causes, and when to see a doctor: explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Does this value suggest an infection, and do I need antibiotics?
Should the value be rechecked in a few weeks?
Could a medication be affecting my white blood cell count?
pageTitle: WBC value: White Blood Cells explained | Medyra
